original_claim: CISA (2024) reports that average PQC migration takes 5 to 10 years.
recommended_claim: CISA (2024) frames PQC adoption as a long transition and emphasizes automated cryptography discovery and inventory tools for tracking migration progress. Do not cite CISA (2024) as the direct source for an average 5 to 10 year migration duration.

document.title: Strategy for Migrating to Automated Post-Quantum Cryptography Discovery and Inventory Tools
document.subject: Migrating to Post-Quantum Cryptography

verified_cisa_2024_points: The strategy supports assessment of Federal Civilian Executive Branch progress toward PQC adoption using automated cryptography discovery and inventory tools.
The strategy supports agency inventories of systems and assets that contain CRQC-vulnerable cryptography and annual submission of inventory to ONCD and CISA.
The strategy says CISA will monitor migration to PQC throughout a long transition period.
The strategy includes planning and reporting milestones across 2023 through 2028 and uses 2035 as a mission-sensitive data reference point.
not_verified_in_cisa_2024: major cryptographic transitions typically take 5 to 10 years
presentation_guidance: Use CISA (2024) as the source for automated discovery and inventory being necessary to track PQC transition progress.
Say that CISA treats PQC migration as a long transition with federal reporting and tooling milestones.
Do not say that CISA (2024) measured or reported an average 5 to 10 year migration duration.
If the presentation keeps a 5 to 10 year estimate, cite it separately as an industry or secondary estimate, not as CISA's 2024 strategy claim.
access_note: The official CISA page and PDF are the citation targets. Local curl access to cisa.gov returned an Akamai 403 during verification, so the PDF text was inspected from a mirrored copy with matching title, author, publication date, metadata, and file size while preserving the official CISA URLs as citation targets.
